name: Seated Dumbbell Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Bent-knee seated position biases the soleus; dumbbells on the knees load a full stretch, hardest at the bottom.
cues: Keep the knee bent about 90 degrees to target the soleus
Deep stretch at the bottom, full squeeze at the top
Pad the knees if the dumbbells are uncomfortable
execution_steps: Sit on a bench with the balls of your feet on a block or plate, heels hanging off, knees bent about 90 degrees.
Rest a dumbbell on top of each knee (or one dumbbell across both), holding them in place with your hands.
Lower your heels toward the floor into a deep calf stretch.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Squeeze the top, then lower slowly to the stretch.
good_when: You want to train the soleus but have no seated calf machine
You train at home with only dumbbells
bad_when: The dumbbells dig into your knees uncomfortably even with padding
You need heavy soleus overload beyond what balancing dumbbells allows
common_errors: Half reps that skip the stretch
Straightening the knees, which shifts work to the gastrocnemius
progressions: Add a pause at the bottom stretch
